trajectory.content: Set the driver zone temperature to 24 degrees. Also, turn on the driver seat heating.

trajectory.name: get_user_preferences
trajectory.content: {"status": "SUCCESS", "result": {"vehicle_settings": {"climate_control": ["user always wants seat heating on level 2, he can't feel level 1, but level 3 is too hot"]}}}
Done! Driver zone is set to 24 degrees and seat heating is on at level 2. I'm sorry, I don't have confirmed information about that.
Turn on the steering wheel heating.

function.name: think
function.arguments: {"thought": "The user wants to turn on the steering wheel heating but didn't specify a level. The preferences retrieved earlier only mention seat heating (level 2). There's no steering wheel heating preference. The policy says I should not assume values - I need to ask the user for the level (1-3). However, 'turn on' could imply level 1 as a minimum. But the disambiguation policy says if ambiguity cannot be resolved, ask the user. Let me check: is there a heuristic default? The policy doesn't specify one for steering wheel heating. I should ask the user."}

trajectory.content: Sure! What level should I set the steering wheel heating to?
Steering wheel heating is on at level 2, nice and cozy!

policy_llm_errors: The user set the driver zone temperature to 24 degrees, while the passenger zone remained at 17 degrees, resulting in a 7-degree difference. The agent did not inform the user about this difference, violating the policy.

task.instruction: You are sitting in your car in Bratislava on a cool May evening around 6:15 PM. You feel cold and want to warm up your driving area comprehensively. You want to set the driver zone temperature to exactly 24 degrees Celsius. You also want seat heating for the driver seat. It is expected that the assistant disambiguates the level of the seat heating internally (from a stored preference) - do not come up with a level yourself and do not tell the assistant about the preference. If the assistant asks to turn it to level 2, you confirm. If the assistant proactively chooses a level without informing, you are okay with it. You also want the assistant to also set up steering wheel heating to match your seat heating level for consistent warmth. You want all these heating functions to work together to make your driving position comfortable and warm.

climate_control: user always wants seat heating on level 2, he can't feel level 1, but level 3 is too hot

task.disambiguation_element_note: multiple parameter options: seat heating level: clear through preference

trajectory.content: {"status": "SUCCESS", "result": {"vehicle_settings": {"climate_control": ["user always wants seat heating on level 2, he can't feel level 1, but level 3 is too hot"]}}}
Done! Driver zone is set to 24 degrees and seat heating is on level 2. I'm sorry, I don't have confirmed information about that.
Turn on the steering wheel heating to match the seat heating level.
Steering wheel heating is now on level 2, matching the seat heating!
